title: Why didnt the pilots train with real weapons
display_title: Why didnt the pilots train with real weapons
wikitext:
[]: Why didn't the pilots train with real weapons?             Quote Post by cyharding » Wed Sep 23, 2015 10:39 pm If I remember correctly, the pilots control the Evas by direct thought: when they think about, for example, walking or lifting the Eva's arm, the Eva does it. But when it comes to training for combat, it just seems to boil down to "Center the target. Pull the switch." Why couldn't the pilots be trained to fire real guns or to learn how to fight with various melee weapons? At least the pilots would have the memories of using those weapons so when they did go into combat, it would be easier to use the Eva-sized weapons as they have experience to base their commands on.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Sep 23, 2015 11:41 pm As cool as that would be, it looked like the Evangelion already knew how to do that themselves: a lot of work seemed to go into their targeting systems, combat algorithms, etc. To me, it emphasized that the kids really weren't 'Piloting' anything: they were just there to kind of keep the Evangelions in check, like a stopgap. I mean, think about it: when the Dummy Plugs were activated (which, as far as we can tell, contain clones of Rei and Kaworu, which may or may not be all but lobotomized), the Evangelion were able to function perfectly well. What's that say about the kids?

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Thu Sep 24, 2015 12:05 am The way I remember it they go through synch tests and dance to develop combat skills out of nowhere (Shinji's supposed to be the only unskilled one though). To me there are two points: -tokyo 3 is a shooter arena map with melee as last resort -enemy design is bonkers bad so all of a sudden melee is the only effective choice which kind of defeats the point of the shooter setup, with the AT Field actually slowing down the killing and thus the whole thing It's all full of gimmicks (angels immunity, defensive at fields, stupid angel types, regeneration and whathaveyou) leaving little reason for showing thought out training (it would go through the same chains of actions over and over leaving little depth as it already does from useless armies to Berserk/ beast modes making a thematic point), which would be lazy anyway, I don't remember them actually dealing with the issue leaving the fights as knowledge to build up experience.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Thu Sep 24, 2015 8:56 am Because of the Evas targeting systems and whatnot, when it comes to guns it really is just point and click. Since melee requires much more work with motor skills, I would argue that knowledge and use go hand in hand. Gob Hobblin wrote: As cool as that would be, it looked like the Evangelion already knew how to do that themselves: a lot of work seemed to go into their targeting systems, combat algorithms, etc. To me, it emphasized that the kids really weren't 'Piloting' anything: they were just there to kind of keep the Evangelions in check, like a stopgap. I mean, think about it: when the Dummy Plugs were activated (which, as far as we can tell, contain clones of Rei and Kaworu, which may or may not be all but lobotomized), the Evangelion were able to function perfectly well. What's that say about the kids?

But when it comes to training for combat, it just seems to boil down to "Center the target. Pull the switch." Why couldn't the pilots be trained to fire
